Title: An iron (II) dependent oxygenase performs the last missing step of plant lysine catabolism

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DUF1338 family; vicinal oxygen chelate (VOC) superfamily; two-substrate α-ketoacid-dependent oxygenase (2S-αKAO) subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream; last previously missing step before 2HG formation from 2OA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 2-oxoadipate to D-2-hydroxyglutarate through Fe(II)- and O2-dependent decarboxylation coupled to intramolecular hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes formation of 2HG from 2OA, defining the missing reaction in lysine catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Intermediate in plant and bacterial lysine catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystal structures of apo and 2OA-bound enzyme; in vitro enzyme assays; oxygen-consumption stoichiometry; 18O2 labeling LC-HRMS; substrate specificity panel; site-directed mutagenesis (R74A, V402P)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing step established; rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro Fe(II)- and O2-dependent assay conditions; 18O2 atmosphere for labeling experiments
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to suppress lysine catabolism when developing high-lysine crops, though direct crop deployment was not tested
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HglS is a DUF1338 Fe(II)-dependent oxygenase that converts 2OA to D-2HG. 2. Structural and isotope-labeling analyses support a decarboxylation-hydroxylation mechanism using molecular oxygen. 3. Arg74 is essential for 2OA specificity and catalytic activity, supporting conserved function across DUF1338 homologs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structures deposited as PDB 6W1G and 6W1H. Conserved metal-binding residues are His70, His226, and Glu294; Arg74 mediates substrate specificity; V402 contributes to substrate binding.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Salk_103299C mutant background for phenotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DUF1338 family; vicinal oxygen chelate (VOC) superfamily; putative chloroplast-targeted 2S-αKAO enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of 2-oxoadipate to 2-hydroxyglutarate in vitro, analogous to bacterial HglS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports lysine catabolism; disruption is associated with delayed germination, chlorosis, impaired growth, and altered seed oil body morphology consistent with lysine catabolism defects
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed with known lysine catabolic enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly coexpressed with known lysine catabolic enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with LC-HRMS; transcript coexpression analysis; T-DNA mutant phenotyping (Salk_103299C); seed histology
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing step established for plant lysine catabolism; rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed germination assays on media and soil under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcript coexpression analysis using ATTED-II; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for reducing lysine catabolism in engineering high-lysine plants, but caution is needed because loss of function is associated with severe germination and developmental defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AT1G07040 encodes a plant DUF1338 enzyme with in vitro 2OA-to-2HG activity. 2. Its expression is highly correlated with known lysine catabolic genes in Arabidopsis. 3. Disruption causes delayed germination and developmental defects consistent with impaired lysine catabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted chloroplast localization sequence; authors note mutant phenotype is preliminary and should be validated with multiple independent T-DNA lines.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DUF1338 family; vicinal oxygen chelate (VOC) superfamily; chloroplast/amyloplast-targeted 2S-αKAO enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of 2-oxoadipate to 2-hydroxyglutarate with activity and substrate-binding mode matching bacterial HglS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports lysine catabolism in rice; prior mutant studies showed floury starch/endosperm defects associated with lysine-related phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Amyloplast via N-terminal chloroplast localization signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with LC-HRMS; kinetic analysis; X-ray crystal structure of substrate-bound protein
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing step established for plant lysine catabolism; rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant target for balancing lysine enhancement and seed quality traits in cereals; disruption may impair starch/endosperm development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rice FLO7 is a DUF1338 enzyme that converts 2OA to 2HG in vitro. 2. FLO7 adopts a conserved active-site architecture despite only ~15% sequence identity with HglS. 3. Prior FLO7 mutant phenotypes support a physiological role in seed lysine catabolism and normal amyloplast development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Structure deposited as PDB 6W1K; Arg64 is the conserved substrate-binding arginine analogous to HglS Arg74; reported kinetic parameters include Km 0.55 mM and Vmax 0.89 mM/min/µM enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DUF1338 family; vicinal oxygen chelate (VOC) superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of 2-oxoadipate to 2-hydroxyglutarate in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Biochemically shows identical activity to HglS, supporting conserved DUF1338 function in bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with high-resolution LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YdcJ is a DUF1338 homolog with HglS-like activity on 2OA. 2. Its activity supports broad conservation of the DUF1338-catalyzed lysine catabolic step in bacteria. 3. Physiological role in E. coli requires further study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Related structure referenced as PDB 2RJB in prior work; included in Results as a biochemically characterized homolog.
